Description: Rahab tied the scarlet cord in her window so her household would be spared when Jericho fell.
Canaanite woman who hid Israel’s spies on her roof and later joined Israel.
The Jordan stopped flowing, piling up in a heap so Israel crossed on dry ground.
Stones taken from the riverbed as a memorial of the miraculous crossing.
First Israelite encampment in Canaan, east of Jericho.
Israel renewed circumcision at Gilgal after 40 wilderness years.
Manna stopped the day Israel ate Canaan’s produce.
Mysterious warrior with drawn sword who met Joshua near Jericho.
After seven days of marching and shouting, Jericho’s walls collapsed.
Priests carrying rams’ horn trumpets led the march around Jericho.
On the seventh day Israel circled Jericho seven times before shouting.
Rahab and all her household were brought to safety because of the scarlet cord.
Joshua cursed anyone who would rebuild Jericho’s foundations.
Achan took a cloak, silver, and gold from Jericho, bringing defeat on Israel.
Place where Achan and his family were stoned for theft.
Israel used a feigned retreat and ambush to capture Ai the second time.
Joshua built an altar of uncut stones on Ebal as Moses commanded.
Half Israel stood before Gerizim, half before Ebal while the Law was read.
Gibeonites used stale bread to pretend they came from far away.
Joshua made the deceptive Gibeonites woodcutters and water carriers forever.
Joshua prayed and the sun halted over Gibeon for about a whole day.
Amorite kings hid in Makkedah cave; Joshua sealed and later executed them.
Joshua made Israel’s captains place feet on the necks of defeated kings.
More enemies died from hailstones than by Israel’s swords.
Only major city Joshua completely burned; its king was killed.
After all major battles, the land finally rested from war.
85-year-old Caleb asked for the hill country with Anakim giants.
Caleb expelled Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai, the three sons of Anak.
Caleb’s daughter asked and received upper and lower springs.
Six appointed cities where accidental manslayers could flee for safety.
Joshua’s famous challenge to Israel to serve the Lord alone.

Phrase: As for me and my house
Description: Joshua declared his household would serve the Lord regardless.
Large stone under the oak that “heard” Israel’s covenant renewal.
Faithful leader buried in his inheritance at Timnath-serah.
Bones brought from Egypt finally buried at Shechem.
Joshua secretly sent two spies from Shittim to view the land.
Rahab hid the spies among stalks of flax on her roof.
Jericho’s king demanded Rahab bring out the Israelite spies.
Priests bearing the ark stood in the riverbed until all crossed.
Name “Gilgal” given because God removed Israel’s shame at circumcision.
First Passover in Canaan kept on the 14th day in Jericho plains.
Commander told Joshua to remove sandals because the place was holy.
Everything in Jericho devoted to the Lord for total destruction except Rahab.
Precious metals from Jericho placed in the treasury of the Lord’s house.
Israel fled from Ai; about 36 men died because of Achan’s sin.
Joshua and elders fell before the ark in mourning after Ai defeat.
Tribe, clan, and household narrowed by lot until Achan was taken.
Achan confessed seeing, coveting, and taking Babylonian cloak and silver.
Joshua stretched out javelin; ambush rose and captured Ai.
Leaders tasted Gibeonite provisions but failed to inquire of the Lord.
Ancient record that mentions the sun standing still at Joshua’s prayer.
Five Amorite kings executed and hung on trees until evening.
Joshua annihilated the giants from the hill country except in Gaza, Gath, Ashdod.
Othniel captured Kiriath-sepher and received Caleb’s daughter as wife.
Levites received the Lord Himself as inheritance instead of territory.
Transjordan tribes named their altar “Witness” to prove unity with Israel.
God’s repeated command to Joshua after Moses’ death.
Rahab told Jericho officials the spies had already left the city.
Priests carrying the ark led Israel across the Jordan.
Stones to remind children how the Jordan was cut off before the ark.
Commander said he came as captain of the Lord’s host, not taking sides.
Jericho utterly destroyed—men, women, young, old, oxen, sheep, donkeys.
After Ai defeat, the hearts of the people melted and became water.
Joshua said Achan troubled Israel, so Israel troubled him with stones.
Six tribes on Gerizim for blessing, six on Ebal for curse.
Jerusalem’s king formed coalition of five Amorite kings against Gibeon.
Joshua commanded the moon to stand still over the Valley of Aijalon.
Joshua ordered large stones rolled against the cave mouth to trap kings.
King Horam came to help Lachish and was utterly destroyed.
Joshua took the entire land, just as the Lord told Moses.
Caleb declared he was as strong at 85 as at 40.
Hebron formerly called Kiriath-arba after Arba, greatest of Anakim.
Zelophehad’s daughters received inheritance among their uncles.
Joshua cast lots for tribes at Shiloh in the presence of the Lord.
Israel set up the tent of meeting at Shiloh after conquest.
Seven tribes had not yet received their inheritance and were delaying.
When Joshua was old, God said much land still needed conquering.
Joshua blessed and dismissed Reuben, Gad, and half-Manasseh.
Western tribes prepared war over the large altar by the Jordan.
Phinehas and ten chiefs confronted eastern tribes about the altar.
Joshua warned Israel they could not serve the Lord because He is jealous.
Israel repeatedly vowed to serve the Lord and put away foreign gods.
Joshua warned that if they forsook God, He would consume them.
Joshua made the people witness against themselves that they chose the Lord.
Joshua commanded Israel to put away foreign gods among them.
Joshua made a covenant and statute for Israel at Shechem.
Aaron’s son Eleazar died and was buried in hill country of Ephraim.
God commanded Joshua to be strong and very courageous to obey the Law.
Joshua told to meditate on the Book of the Law day and night.
Spies swore by the Lord to spare Rahab’s family for her kindness.
Officers commanded people to prepare and follow the ark after three days.
Joshua told people to consecrate because tomorrow the Lord would do wonders.
People told to keep distance from ark since they had never passed this way.
Moses swore Caleb would inherit the land his foot trod because he followed fully.
The Lord drove out great nations; no one could withstand Joshua.
Joshua urged Israel to continue clinging to the Lord as they had done.
Because the Lord fought for Israel, one man could chase a thousand.
Joshua warned intermarriage would become snare, whip, and thorns in eyes.
Israel served the Lord throughout Joshua’s life and the elders who outlived him.
After Joshua and elders died, another generation arose who did not know the Lord.
